text: fat is 47.4g. omega3 is where things start to fall off. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
carbs is 15.3g. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for a seed, that is exactly what you want to see.
protein is 28.3g. essential amino acid support is one of the better parts here. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
vitamin e at 1% DV, vitamin b12 at 0% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 65% DV, zinc at 54% DV, iron at 38% DV stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

pros first. unusually protein-forward for a seed snack because that gives it some practical appeal versus lighter snack foods. has clear crunchy snack appeal because easy to understand and package as a snack food. still brings some useful minerals because not devoid of nutritional upside despite the weaker category fit.

the drawbacks next. salt load is a major practical negative because the roasted salted format brings a clear real-world downside. weak fibre density for the seed category because seeds are supposed to dominate on fibre more convincingly than this. fat profile is rough for a seed-category food because too much saturated fat and not enough omega-3 support for this category.
D tier. Best only as a supporting add-on rather than something to lean on heavily.
